goal: Close BUG-003 (stale cardOfTheDay slot) as not-reproducible after verifying the safeguard already exists
done: Updated BUG-003 status to Closed — not reproducible as filed; appended closing note pointing at js/today.js:92 (date-equality check landed in commit acb00537 on 2026-04-27, five days before BUG-003 was filed). No source-code change.
symptom: BUG-003 record claimed today.js renders cardOfTheDay without comparing stored date to today's local date, would display yesterday's card across day boundaries
root_cause: BUG-003 record was filed without tracing the only consumer of readCardOfTheDay() — renderToday() at js/today.js:85. That consumer already does the date-equality check at line 92 and falls through to the #today-empty branch (which exposes a 'Draw your card for today' CTA to /daily.html). Stale-slot scenario is already handled.
fix: docs-only: status changed to Closed — not reproducible as filed; closing note explains the existing safeguard and the moot product decision
verified: repo-wide grep for readCardOfTheDay and DAILY_CARD_OF_DAY_STORAGE_KEY callers
notes: If a different stale-display symptom surfaces on iPhone (service-worker cache, timezone edge case in toLocalDateIso, etc.), file as a new bug with concrete repro steps — do not reopen BUG-003.
